rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the specified component implementation when the requirement binds to implementation-level behavior.
Preserve the AADL architecture structure by not modifying component types, subcomponents, or connections during fusion.
generic_rules: Place interface-level assumptions and guarantees in component types; place implementation-specific assignments in component implementations.
Only reference ports or data elements explicitly defined in the AADL model—never infer or invent variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use the syntax: annex agree {** ... **};
Implementation annexes must appear after subcomponents and connections in the implementation body.
anti_patterns: Do not convert AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) into AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
Avoid imposing numeric bounds (e.g., x >= 0) unless explicitly stated in the requirement and supported by the AADL type system.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
